title: Docker Build Cache Efficiency and CI Pipeline Throughput
exclusions: Does not replace registry pull failure classification, rate-limit burn-down, or TLS and DNS diagnostics covered by UC-3.1.26. Excludes runtime-only container CPU or memory signals after workloads start, Kubernetes admission vulnerability decisions, per-CVE triage, pure language-toolchain unit tests that never invoke docker build, and developer laptop builds that never forward telemetry to governed indexes.
evidence: Saved search container_uc_3_1_18_build_cache_ci_throughput; versioned ci_build_sla.csv in git with commit hash captured in weekly evidence exports; dashboard drilldowns linking docker:build lines, buildkit:metadata JSON, and buildkit:prometheus series for the same pipeline_id.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab repo, run two consecutive warm docker build jobs with BuildKit metadata forwarded, then reorder an early Dockerfile COPY to force a cache miss, ingest all events into index=oti_containers, execute the saved search, and expect CACHE-DEGRADED or REGRESSION with lower avg_cache_hit_pct versus the prior rolling window while pipeline_id remains traceable.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Ingest only healthy completion events where cache_hit_pct stays above the lookup floor, build_total_sec remains below build_sec_budget, and drop_vs_baseline_pct stays below thirty; confirm severity remains OK and action_required equals none.

dataSources: index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:build for structured docker build --progress=plain line streams or aggregated step lines (CACHED versus executed RUN, COPY, ADD) forwarded from CI workers; sourcetype=buildkit:metadata for BuildKit --metadata-file JSON summaries (total steps, cache hits, durations, cache import intervals, optional cache volume sizes); sourcetype IN (gha:workflow_job, gitlab:ci:job, jenkins:build:finished, tekton:pipelinerun) for job completion, queue wait, runner identity, and wall-clock segments; sourcetype=buildkit:prometheus for Prometheus exposition scraped from a BuildKit daemon or remote builder (buildkit_* metric families including cache volume hit ratio and size); sourcetype=ci:runner:agent for runner saturation gauges (queued jobs, in-flight executors, CPU steal) when the agent exports JSON or OTLP-derived logs; subsearch inputlookup ci_build_sla.csv supplying repo, branch, build_sec_budget, cache_pct_floor, and accountable owner for SLA math.
app: Custom HEC input (BuildKit --metadata-file JSON, docker build --progress=plain, GitHub Actions or GitLab CI or Jenkins or Tekton completion webhooks, BuildKit Prometheus scrape via Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus receiver) as the primary path; Splunk Add-on for Docker (Splunk_TA_docker) optional where the same index already ingests engine and builder-adjacent context from managed hosts.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.1.18 Docker Build Cache Efficiency and CI Pipeline Throughput. Tunables: index=oti_containers; earliest=-7d@d latest=now; inputlookup ci_build_sla.csv on repo branch; default build_sec_budget=600 cache_pct_floor=45 when lookup missing; streamstats time_window=604800 requires time-ordered events per repo branch.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:build" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval repo=trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, github_repository, gitlab_project_path, "unknown_repo")))
      | eval branch=trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, head_ref, ref, "unknown_branch")))
      | eval build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(build_id, build_uuid, buildkit_session, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval cached_step_line=if(match(lr, "(?i)^#\\d+\\s+.*\\bcached\\b.*(run|copy|add)"), 1, 0)
      | eval exec_step_line=if(match(lr, "(?i)^#\\d+\\s+.*\\]\\s+(run|copy|add)\\s") AND NOT match(lr, "(?i)cached"), 1, 0)
      | stats sum(cached_step_line) AS cache_hits sum(exec_step_line) AS exec_steps max(_time) AS last_event_time BY repo branch build_id
      | eval total_steps=cache_hits + exec_steps
      | eval cache_hit_pct=if(total_steps > 0, round(100.0 * cache_hits / total_steps, 2), null())
      | eval build_duration_sec=null()
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=null()
      | eval queue_seconds=null()
      | eval runner_id=""
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=null()
      | eval gha_run_id=""
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=""
      | eval jenkins_build_id=""
      | eval lane="docker_plain" ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="buildkit:metadata" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval repo=trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, "unknown_repo")))
      | eval branch=trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, "unknown_branch")))
      | eval build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(build_id, session_id, buildkit_session, "")))
      | eval cache_hits=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_hits, cacheHits, hits, "0")), 10)
      | eval total_steps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(total_steps, steps, "0")), 10)
      | eval cache_hit_pct=if(total_steps > 0, round(100.0 * cache_hits / total_steps, 2), null())
      | eval build_duration_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(build_duration_sec, duration_seconds, build_seconds, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_pull_seconds, cache_import_duration_sec, registry_cache_pull_sec, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_volume_size_mb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_size_mb, cache_mount_mb, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=if(isnotnull(cache_volume_size_mb), round(cache_volume_size_mb / 1024.0, 3), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_size_gb, "")), 10))
      | eval queue_seconds=null()
      | eval runner_id=""
      | eval last_event_time=_time
      | eval gha_run_id=trim(toString(coalesce(gha_run_id, github_run_id, "")))
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=trim(toString(coalesce(gitlab_pipeline_id, "")))
      | eval jenkins_build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(jenkins_build_id, "")))
      | eval lane="buildkit_json" ]
    [ search index=oti_containers (sourcetype="gha:workflow_job" OR sourcetype="gitlab:ci:job" OR sourcetype="jenkins:build:finished" OR sourcetype="tekton:pipelinerun") earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval repo=trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, github_repository, gitlab_project_path, "unknown_repo")))
      | eval branch=trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, head_ref, ref, "unknown_branch")))
      | eval gha_run_id=trim(toString(coalesce(github_run_id, gha_run_id, run_id, "")))
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=trim(toString(coalesce(gitlab_pipeline_id, pipeline_id, "")))
      | eval jenkins_build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(jenkins_build_id, build_number, "")))
      | eval build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(build_id, pipeline_run_uid, "")))
      | eval pipeline_id=coalesce(build_id, gha_run_id, gitlab_pipeline_id, jenkins_build_id)
      | eval queue_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_seconds, queue_time_sec, waiting_seconds, queued_duration_sec, "0")), 10)
      | eval build_duration_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(job_duration_sec, duration_sec, build_duration_sec, wall_seconds, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_restore_seconds, actions_cache_download_sec, "")), 10)
      | eval runner_id=trim(toString(coalesce(runner_name, runner_id, agent_name, executor_label, "")))
      | eval cache_hit_pct=null()
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=null()
      | eval last_event_time=_time
      | eval lane="ci_job" ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="buildkit:prometheus" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval metric_name=lower(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, "")))
      | where match(metric_name, "buildkit_.*")
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, instance, kubernetes_pod_name, "")))
      | eval cache_volume_hit_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_hit_ratio, le_1, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_size_gb, value, "")), 10)
      | eval runner_cpu_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(runner_cpu_pct, process_cpu_percent, "")), 10)
      | eval repo="buildkit_exporter"
      | eval branch=host_id
      | eval build_id=host_id
      | eval pipeline_id=host_id
      | eval cache_hit_pct=if(isnotnull(cache_volume_hit_ratio), round(100.0 * cache_volume_hit_ratio, 2), null())
      | eval build_duration_sec=null()
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=null()
      | eval queue_seconds=null()
      | eval runner_id=host_id
      | eval last_event_time=_time
      | eval gha_run_id=""
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=""
      | eval jenkins_build_id=""
      | eval lane="bk_metrics" ]
| eval pipeline_id=trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_id, build_id, gha_run_id, gitlab_pipeline_id, jenkins_build_id, "unknown_pipeline")))
| eval build_total_sec=coalesce(build_duration_sec, 0) + coalesce(cache_pull_seconds, 0) + coalesce(queue_seconds, 0)
| stats
    avg(cache_hit_pct) AS avg_cache_hit_pct
    avg(build_total_sec) AS avg_build_total_sec
    median(cache_pull_seconds) AS cache_pull_seconds
    median(queue_seconds) AS queue_seconds
    latest(runner_id) AS runner_id
    latest(cache_volume_size_gb) AS cache_volume_size_gb
    max(last_event_time) AS last_build_time
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
  BY repo branch pipeline_id
| where len(pipeline_id) > 3 AND NOT match(pipeline_id, "^unknown$")
| eval repo=lower(repo)
| eval branch=lower(branch)
| sort 0 repo branch last_build_time
| streamstats time_window=604800 current=f window=20000 avg(avg_cache_hit_pct) AS avg7d_cache_pct BY repo branch
| eval drop_vs_baseline_pct=if(isnotnull(avg7d_cache_pct) AND avg7d_cache_pct > 1, round(100.0 * (avg7d_cache_pct - avg_cache_hit_pct) / avg7d_cache_pct, 2), null())
| eventstats avg(avg_build_total_sec) AS branch_avg_build_sec BY repo branch
| join type=left max=0 repo branch
    [| inputlookup ci_build_sla.csv
     | eval repo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, ""))))
     | eval branch=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, ""))))
     | eval build_sec_budget=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(build_sec_budget, max_build_seconds_main, max_build_seconds_pr, "600")), 10)
     | eval cache_pct_floor=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_pct_floor, min_cache_hit_pct, "45")), 10)
     | eval owner=trim(toString(coalesce(owner, owner_team, platform_owner, "Head of Platform")))
     | fields repo branch build_sec_budget cache_pct_floor owner ]
| fillnull value=600 build_sec_budget
| fillnull value=45 cache_pct_floor
| fillnull value="Head of Platform" owner
| eval severity=case(
    avg_cache_hit_pct < cache_pct_floor AND avg_build_total_sec > build_sec_budget, "CACHE-COLLAPSE",
    avg_cache_hit_pct < cache_pct_floor, "CACHE-DEGRADED",
    avg_build_total_sec > build_sec_budget, "SLA-BREACH",
    drop_vs_baseline_pct > 30, "REGRESSION",
    true(), "OK")
| eval action_required=case(
    severity="CACHE-COLLAPSE", "freeze_merge_and_reorder_dockerfile_layers_remote_cache",
    severity="CACHE-DEGRADED", "find_first_uncached_instruction_and_dependency_diff",
    severity="SLA-BREACH", "add_runners_or_shard_matrix_and_profile_queue",
    severity="REGRESSION", "diff_last_green_commit_for_cache_key_shift",
    true(), "none")
| eval sev_rank=case(severity="CACHE-COLLAPSE",1, severity="CACHE-DEGRADED",2, severity="SLA-BREACH",3, severity="REGRESSION",4, severity="OK",5, true(),9)
| sort sev_rank, - drop_vs_baseline_pct
| table repo branch pipeline_id avg_cache_hit_pct avg_build_total_sec cache_pull_seconds queue_seconds runner_id cache_volume_size_gb drop_vs_baseline_pct severity build_sec_budget cache_pct_floor owner action_required branch_avg_build_sec
description: This use case monitors the CI build engine and runner plane where Fortune-scale teams lose entire release days to invisible cache collapse: BuildKit stops reusing layers after a single requirements.txt reorder, a monthly ubuntu:24.04 base refresh forces a cold graph, or a registry-backed --cache-from pull adds minutes of import time that never appear in application APM. The control correlates plain-progress step lines, structured BuildKit metadata, CI job completion telemetry, and optional buildkit_* Prometheus series so each row names repository, branch, pipeline identifier, cache-hit percentage, wall-clock seconds including queue and cache restore, runner identity, cache volume growth signals, rolling seven-day cache baseline drop, and severity across CACHE-COLLAPSE, CACHE-DEGRADED, SLA-BREACH, REGRESSION, and OK. Distinction from UC-3.1.26 is explicit: sibling UC-3.1.26 covers north-south registry pull failures, rate limits, and TLS or DNS faults during image download, while UC-3.1.18 covers compile and assembly time inside docker build, cache mount effectiveness, inline versus registry cache backends, and CI executor saturation that lengthens builds even when pulls succeed.
value: Platform economics improve when cache-hit percentage and build wall-clock share one accountable view: FinOps sees fewer wasted vCPU hours from redundant npm or pip reinstalls, developers regain predictable pull-request feedback under eight minutes, and incident reviews stop arguing whether slow deploys came from the registry or from a broken layer graph. Capacity planning gains a quantitative signal for how many warm builders a monorepo truly needs after Dockerfile churn, and security teams can distinguish deliberate cache wipes from silent drift. Steering committees receive evidence that main-branch build budgets and cache floors in ci_build_sla.csv are monitored with the same rigor as production SLOs, which supports audit narratives around change velocity without encouraging unsafe image promotion shortcuts.
implementation: Land docker:build, buildkit:metadata, CI completion sourcetypes, and buildkit:prometheus in index=oti_containers via HEC or OpenTelemetry scraping. Version ci_build_sla.csv in git with repo, branch, build_sec_budget, cache_pct_floor, owner. Save container_uc_3_1_18_build_cache_ci_throughput hourly, route CACHE-COLLAPSE and SLA-BREACH to platform CI on-call, and archive weekly CSV snapshots with lookup hashes.
visualization: Timechart of avg_cache_hit_pct and avg7d_cache_pct per repo with deploy annotations, severity-sorted table with drilldown to docker:build and buildkit:metadata, dual-axis chart of avg_build_total_sec versus queue_seconds, single value of CACHE-COLLAPSE count in last twenty-four hours, heatmap of drop_vs_baseline_pct by branch, and buildkit:prometheus cache volume size trend panel.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(_time) AS last_seen FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.Performance earliest=-7d latest=now BY Performance.dest Performance.process
| rename Performance.dest AS repo Performance.process AS pipeline_hint
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance for normalized build-duration metrics and Inventory for CI runner asset rows when props and tags map repo, branch, and runner_id into dest and user_package fields; retain at least thirty days for weekly cache-regression reviews.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Splunk CIM Performance data model
GitHub Docs — Caching dependencies to speed up workflows
Splunk Observability — OpenTelemetry Collector Prometheus receiver

knownFalsePositives: Monthly or weekly upstream base-image refreshes for tags such as ubuntu:24.04 legitimately collapse cache-hit percentages for one build wave even when engineers did nothing wrong; require comparison against a documented base-image rotation calendar before paging. Large Dockerfile refactors that intentionally reorder layers or merge stages produce sharp CACHE-DEGRADED rows that resemble incidents; pair with change records or a lookup refactor_expected flag. Monorepo pipelines that compile many targets in one job report low cache-hit ratios relative to microservice repos because denominator steps include unrelated artifacts; segment by matrix dimension or accept wider floors for those repos in ci_build_sla.csv. Security teams sometimes wipe builder caches after compromise; expect temporary CACHE-DEGRADED across the fleet until warm cycles complete, and annotate ticket ids in dashboards. Self-hosted runner disk pressure evicts local BuildKit cache with LRU behavior, mimicking cache collapse until volumes resize; correlate with host disk_percent and eviction logs. Runner restarts between build stages drop in-memory cache while registry-backed cache remains; duration may spike once without implying Dockerfile faults—check runner uptime versus build start. Parallel CI matrix builds duplicate pipeline_id families or multiply step counts, inflating apparent REGRESSION frequency when streamstats spans heterogeneous jobs; consolidate identifiers upstream or filter matrix labels. Sparse repos with fewer than two builds inside seven days produce noisy drop_vs_baseline_pct; widen earliest for those branches or fall back to daily summaries. Hosted CI brownouts can raise queue_seconds uniformly; confirm provider status before blaming layer cache. Prometheus cardinality or scrape gaps can nullify bk_metrics arms while plain progress still looks healthy; do not silence CACHE-DEGRADED solely because exporter panels are empty.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — bind per-repo build-duration KPIs to release services and episode when CACHE-COLLAPSE coincides with deploy-queue backlog

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the CI platform engineering lead, the developer experience sponsor who signs per-branch build-time budgets, and the observability engineer who operates OpenTelemetry collectors against BuildKit exporters. UC-3.1.18 is intentionally narrow: it quantifies docker build and BuildKit cache efficiency plus CI runner throughput for pipelines that produce container images. UC-3.1.26 remains the registry pull, Hub rate-limit, and egress health story. UC-3.1.5 and UC-3.1.10 remain vulnerability scanning and CVE triage once an image exists. UC-3.1.14 covers intra-cluster overlay reachability, which does not explain why a docker build step recompiled a static binary. None of those siblings substitute per-build cache-hit ratio math, BuildKit metadata import intervals, or queue_seconds spent waiting for a self-hosted runner label.

Before indexing, define contracts. docker:build events must preserve chronological plain-progress lines or pre-aggregated per-step flags so Splunk can distinguish CACHED lines from executed RUN, COPY, and ADD instructions without false positives from unrelated log text. buildkit:metadata events should carry the JSON object emitted by BuildKit when engineers pass --metadata-file, including stable identifiers for session or build id, repository and branch fields injected by CI wrappers, integer cache hit and step totals, duration seconds, optional cache import durations, and optional cache volume size gauges for mount-type caches. CI completion sourcetypes must share coalesce-friendly fields with multisearch: github_run_id, gitlab_pipeline_id, jenkins_build_number or equivalent, job duration, queue or waiting seconds, runner name or agent label, and repository plus branch strings normalized to lowercase for lookup joins. buildkit:prometheus rows should map __name__ or metric_name, instance or host, and numeric values for cache volume hit ratio and size series documented in upstream BuildKit observability references. ci:runner:agent is optional but valuable when it exports queued versus in-flight executor counts because SLA breaches often trace to runner starvation rather than Dockerfile mistakes.

ci_build_sla.csv lives in git and loads through inputlookup. Minimum columns: repo, branch, build_sec_budget in seconds for wall-clock SLA including queue and cache restore unless your organization separates those in a forked macro, cache_pct_floor as the minimum acceptable rolling cache-hit percentage for warm builds, and owner or owner_team for paging. Optional columns for pipeline_profile or workload_class allow future joins without breaking this search. Refresh the CSV whenever main and pull-request budgets diverge or when FinOps approves new runner pools.

RBAC restricts raw build logs to platform and service teams because plain progress can echo private package names or internal hostnames. HEC tokens stay in vault with rotation. Redact secrets at ingest when engineers pass build-args that should never be searchable.

Risk briefing: a green registry path plus a failing cache strategy still burns thousands of vCPU hours per week. Auditors who ask only about image scanning miss the operational resilience story unless build-time SLAs appear beside vulnerability SLAs.

Licensing and volume: plain progress streams are chatty during parallel builds; route experimental namespaces to non-production indexes with macros that still allow multisearch during investigations. Metadata JSON is comparatively small. Prometheus scrapes should respect cardinality guardrails; drop high-card labels at the collector when builder fleets scale past practical limits.

Differentiation recap: this UC does not replace registry egress monitoring, runtime syscall detections, or admission-time vulnerability gates. It is the build-cache and CI throughput plane.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Configure GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, or Tekton to emit completion events into index=oti_containers with sourcetype locked per platform family. Wrap docker build with --progress=plain or an equivalent structured logger that forwards each line or batched lines to HEC with sourcetype=docker:build. Enable BuildKit and add --metadata-file on supported builders, posting the resulting JSON as sourcetype=buildkit:metadata immediately after the build exits so durations and cache statistics align with the same build_id your CI logs already carry. For GitHub Actions, include workflow name, job name, run attempt, and runner name fields. For GitLab, include project path, pipeline id, job id, and runner manager hostname when available. For Jenkins, include build number, node name, and queue duration extracted from build listeners. For Tekton, include PipelineRun uid and resolved params that identify git revision and target image.

Install Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector on a metrics gateway or on builder subnets where security approves outbound scrape. Configure a prometheus receiver against BuildKit or remote builder /metrics endpoints, mapping series into sourcetype=buildkit:prometheus with host or instance dimensions preserved. Align scrape intervals with CI frequency: fifteen to thirty seconds is typical. Document which metrics back cache_volume_hit_ratio and cache_volume_size_gb because exporter revisions rename series occasionally.

Optional Splunk Add-on for Docker on administrative hosts can enrich context when the same index already receives engine-level events, but the primary path remains HEC for CI because builders are ephemeral.

props.conf and transforms.conf should timestamp events at line emission or build completion consistently. Use LINE_BREAKER rules that keep #numbered docker lines intact. For JSON metadata, use indexed extractions with consistent field names across branches.

Validate on a canary repository: run one warm build twice, confirm the second build shows higher cache_hit_pct in Splunk, inject a trivial Dockerfile reorder, confirm cache_hit_pct drops and lanes_seen includes docker_plain or buildkit_json, and verify CI completion events carry non-zero queue_seconds when you temporarily constrain runner count.

Security hygiene: never ship registry passwords or short-lived tokens inside _raw. Strip HTTP Authorization headers from exporter debug logs. Document air-gapped cache import latency separately because cache_pull_seconds will reflect mirror throughput, not only Docker Hub.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as saved search container_uc_3_1_18_build_cache_ci_throughput with hourly schedule over earliest=-7d@d latest=now for executive rollups, and a fifteen-minute variant filtered to main or release branches if volume requires. Throttle duplicate REGRESSION rows per repo branch for two hours unless severity escalates to CACHE-COLLAPSE or SLA-BREACH.

Pipeline walkthrough for operators: the opening comment macro lists index, sourcetype families, lookup name, default budgets, and reminds that streamstats time_window=604800 expects events sorted by time within each repo branch partition. multisearch fans four arms so silent plain logs do not hide rich metadata, and so Prometheus cache volume signals still surface when CI forgets to upload metadata files.

The docker_plain arm approximates cache hits versus executed steps using line patterns that match numbered BuildKit or legacy docker build output. Adjust rex patterns in a macro if your organization standardizes on buildx nested output. The buildkit_json arm prefers authoritative totals when CI emits metadata. The ci_job arm supplies queue_seconds and wall-clock segments plus runner identity; coalesce maps GitHub, GitLab, and Jenkins identifiers into pipeline_id. The bk_metrics arm surfaces daemon-level cache volume hit ratio and size; repo and branch are synthetic keys so those rows participate without inventing fake git metadata.

After fan-in, eval build_total_sec sums build_duration_sec, cache_pull_seconds, and queue_seconds so SLA breaches reflect waiting and cache restore, not only compiler time. stats aggregates per repo branch pipeline_id with medians for long-tail stability. streamstats time_window=604800 computes avg7d_cache_pct for rolling seven-day behavior. drop_vs_baseline_pct measures sharp regression versus that rolling average. join type=left max=0 applies governance budgets from ci_build_sla.csv with fillnull defaults when a new branch is not yet listed.

The severity case ladder follows CACHE-COLLAPSE when both cache and duration fail, CACHE-DEGRADED when only cache fails, SLA-BREACH when duration alone fails, REGRESSION when rolling drop exceeds thirty percent with otherwise passing absolute thresholds, and OK as default. action_required encodes concise runbook verbs for paging bridges.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.1.18 Docker Build Cache Efficiency and CI Pipeline Throughput. Tunables: index=oti_containers; earliest=-7d@d latest=now; inputlookup ci_build_sla.csv on repo branch; default build_sec_budget=600 cache_pct_floor=45 when lookup missing; streamstats time_window=604800 requires time-ordered events per repo branch.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:build" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval repo=trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, github_repository, gitlab_project_path, "unknown_repo")))
      | eval branch=trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, head_ref, ref, "unknown_branch")))
      | eval build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(build_id, build_uuid, buildkit_session, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval cached_step_line=if(match(lr, "(?i)^#\\d+\\s+.*\\bcached\\b.*(run|copy|add)"), 1, 0)
      | eval exec_step_line=if(match(lr, "(?i)^#\\d+\\s+.*\\]\\s+(run|copy|add)\\s") AND NOT match(lr, "(?i)cached"), 1, 0)
      | stats sum(cached_step_line) AS cache_hits sum(exec_step_line) AS exec_steps max(_time) AS last_event_time BY repo branch build_id
      | eval total_steps=cache_hits + exec_steps
      | eval cache_hit_pct=if(total_steps > 0, round(100.0 * cache_hits / total_steps, 2), null())
      | eval build_duration_sec=null()
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=null()
      | eval queue_seconds=null()
      | eval runner_id=""
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=null()
      | eval gha_run_id=""
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=""
      | eval jenkins_build_id=""
      | eval lane="docker_plain" ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="buildkit:metadata" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval repo=trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, "unknown_repo")))
      | eval branch=trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, "unknown_branch")))
      | eval build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(build_id, session_id, buildkit_session, "")))
      | eval cache_hits=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_hits, cacheHits, hits, "0")), 10)
      | eval total_steps=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(total_steps, steps, "0")), 10)
      | eval cache_hit_pct=if(total_steps > 0, round(100.0 * cache_hits / total_steps, 2), null())
      | eval build_duration_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(build_duration_sec, duration_seconds, build_seconds, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_pull_seconds, cache_import_duration_sec, registry_cache_pull_sec, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_volume_size_mb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_size_mb, cache_mount_mb, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=if(isnotnull(cache_volume_size_mb), round(cache_volume_size_mb / 1024.0, 3), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_size_gb, "")), 10))
      | eval queue_seconds=null()
      | eval runner_id=""
      | eval last_event_time=_time
      | eval gha_run_id=trim(toString(coalesce(gha_run_id, github_run_id, "")))
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=trim(toString(coalesce(gitlab_pipeline_id, "")))
      | eval jenkins_build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(jenkins_build_id, "")))
      | eval lane="buildkit_json" ]
    [ search index=oti_containers (sourcetype="gha:workflow_job" OR sourcetype="gitlab:ci:job" OR sourcetype="jenkins:build:finished" OR sourcetype="tekton:pipelinerun") earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval repo=trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, github_repository, gitlab_project_path, "unknown_repo")))
      | eval branch=trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, head_ref, ref, "unknown_branch")))
      | eval gha_run_id=trim(toString(coalesce(github_run_id, gha_run_id, run_id, "")))
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=trim(toString(coalesce(gitlab_pipeline_id, pipeline_id, "")))
      | eval jenkins_build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(jenkins_build_id, build_number, "")))
      | eval build_id=trim(toString(coalesce(build_id, pipeline_run_uid, "")))
      | eval pipeline_id=coalesce(build_id, gha_run_id, gitlab_pipeline_id, jenkins_build_id)
      | eval queue_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_seconds, queue_time_sec, waiting_seconds, queued_duration_sec, "0")), 10)
      | eval build_duration_sec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(job_duration_sec, duration_sec, build_duration_sec, wall_seconds, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_restore_seconds, actions_cache_download_sec, "")), 10)
      | eval runner_id=trim(toString(coalesce(runner_name, runner_id, agent_name, executor_label, "")))
      | eval cache_hit_pct=null()
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=null()
      | eval last_event_time=_time
      | eval lane="ci_job" ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="buildkit:prometheus" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval metric_name=lower(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, "")))
      | where match(metric_name, "buildkit_.*")
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, instance, kubernetes_pod_name, "")))
      | eval cache_volume_hit_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_hit_ratio, le_1, "")), 10)
      | eval cache_volume_size_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_volume_size_gb, value, "")), 10)
      | eval runner_cpu_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(runner_cpu_pct, process_cpu_percent, "")), 10)
      | eval repo="buildkit_exporter"
      | eval branch=host_id
      | eval build_id=host_id
      | eval pipeline_id=host_id
      | eval cache_hit_pct=if(isnotnull(cache_volume_hit_ratio), round(100.0 * cache_volume_hit_ratio, 2), null())
      | eval build_duration_sec=null()
      | eval cache_pull_seconds=null()
      | eval queue_seconds=null()
      | eval runner_id=host_id
      | eval last_event_time=_time
      | eval gha_run_id=""
      | eval gitlab_pipeline_id=""
      | eval jenkins_build_id=""
      | eval lane="bk_metrics" ]
| eval pipeline_id=trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_id, build_id, gha_run_id, gitlab_pipeline_id, jenkins_build_id, "unknown_pipeline")))
| eval build_total_sec=coalesce(build_duration_sec, 0) + coalesce(cache_pull_seconds, 0) + coalesce(queue_seconds, 0)
| stats
    avg(cache_hit_pct) AS avg_cache_hit_pct
    avg(build_total_sec) AS avg_build_total_sec
    median(cache_pull_seconds) AS cache_pull_seconds
    median(queue_seconds) AS queue_seconds
    latest(runner_id) AS runner_id
    latest(cache_volume_size_gb) AS cache_volume_size_gb
    max(last_event_time) AS last_build_time
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
  BY repo branch pipeline_id
| where len(pipeline_id) > 3 AND NOT match(pipeline_id, "^unknown$")
| eval repo=lower(repo)
| eval branch=lower(branch)
| sort 0 repo branch last_build_time
| streamstats time_window=604800 current=f window=20000 avg(avg_cache_hit_pct) AS avg7d_cache_pct BY repo branch
| eval drop_vs_baseline_pct=if(isnotnull(avg7d_cache_pct) AND avg7d_cache_pct > 1, round(100.0 * (avg7d_cache_pct - avg_cache_hit_pct) / avg7d_cache_pct, 2), null())
| eventstats avg(avg_build_total_sec) AS branch_avg_build_sec BY repo branch
| join type=left max=0 repo branch
    [| inputlookup ci_build_sla.csv
     | eval repo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(repo, repository, ""))))
     | eval branch=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(branch, git_branch, ""))))
     | eval build_sec_budget=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(build_sec_budget, max_build_seconds_main, max_build_seconds_pr, "600")), 10)
     | eval cache_pct_floor=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cache_pct_floor, min_cache_hit_pct, "45")), 10)
     | eval owner=trim(toString(coalesce(owner, owner_team, platform_owner, "Head of Platform")))
     | fields repo branch build_sec_budget cache_pct_floor owner ]
| fillnull value=600 build_sec_budget
| fillnull value=45 cache_pct_floor
| fillnull value="Head of Platform" owner
| eval severity=case(
    avg_cache_hit_pct < cache_pct_floor AND avg_build_total_sec > build_sec_budget, "CACHE-COLLAPSE",
    avg_cache_hit_pct < cache_pct_floor, "CACHE-DEGRADED",
    avg_build_total_sec > build_sec_budget, "SLA-BREACH",
    drop_vs_baseline_pct > 30, "REGRESSION",
    true(), "OK")
| eval action_required=case(
    severity="CACHE-COLLAPSE", "freeze_merge_and_reorder_dockerfile_layers_remote_cache",
    severity="CACHE-DEGRADED", "find_first_uncached_instruction_and_dependency_diff",
    severity="SLA-BREACH", "add_runners_or_shard_matrix_and_profile_queue",
    severity="REGRESSION", "diff_last_green_commit_for_cache_key_shift",
    true(), "none")
| eval sev_rank=case(severity="CACHE-COLLAPSE",1, severity="CACHE-DEGRADED",2, severity="SLA-BREACH",3, severity="REGRESSION",4, severity="OK",5, true(),9)
| sort sev_rank, - drop_vs_baseline_pct
| table repo branch pipeline_id avg_cache_hit_pct avg_build_total_sec cache_pull_seconds queue_seconds runner_id cache_volume_size_gb drop_vs_baseline_pct severity build_sec_budget cache_pct_floor owner action_required branch_avg_build_sec
```

Alert actions should include repo, branch, pipeline_id, severity, avg_cache_hit_pct, avg_build_total_sec, drop_vs_baseline_pct, and owner in the notable body. Deep-link to UC-3.1.26 only after confirming registry pull error_class rows are quiet for the same interval.

Step 4 — Validate

Positive path A — warm repeat build: run the same Dockerfile twice on a fixed builder, ingest events, execute the saved search, and expect the second pipeline_id or build_id shows higher avg_cache_hit_pct than the first when metadata or plain lines arrive.

Positive path B — forced miss: touch an early Dockerfile layer such as COPY package.json before dependency install, rebuild, expect CACHE-DEGRADED or lower avg_cache_hit_pct versus the prior seven-day streamstats window when budgets are tight.

Positive path C — SLA breach without cache fault: constrain runners so queue_seconds dominates, expect SLA-BREACH even when cache_hit_pct stays high, confirming build_total_sec sums queue.

Positive path D — metadata only: disable plain forwarding temporarily, ingest only buildkit:metadata, confirm stats still populate avg_cache_hit_pct from the JSON arm.

Negative path — synthetic OK row: ingest aligned low-duration job with high cache_hit_pct inside budget, expect OK severity after join supplies floors.

Field sanity: rename vendor fields to camelCase in lab and verify coalesce lists still populate repo and branch. RBAC: readers without oti_containers must see zero rows. Clock skew: enforce chrony on runners before trusting streamstats windows. Correlation: when severity fires, compare with UC-3.1.26 pull telemetry to avoid misattributing registry latency to cache.

Performance: if multisearch exceeds interactive budgets, schedule an hourly summary that writes per-repo branch pipeline_id aggregates to a summary index and point dashboards at summaries while keeping this search for investigations.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Operationalize with a panel showing avg7d_cache_pct versus latest avg_cache_hit_pct per repo, a severity-sorted table with drilldowns to docker:build raw lines and buildkit:metadata JSON, a secondary panel for buildkit:prometheus cache volume size growth, and annotations when ci_build_sla.csv changes. Archive weekly CSV exports with commit hashes to your evidence index for steering reviews.

Troubleshooting

Case 1 — docker_plain arm always null cache_hit_pct: LINE_BREAKER merged numbered lines or progress format differs from rex expectations; capture a raw sample, update the cached_step_line and exec_step_line patterns in a macro, and replay.

Case 2 — buildkit_json never arrives: CI workflow omits --metadata-file or uploads artifact only to object storage; add a post-build curl HEC step or OTel log exporter bound to the metadata path.

Case 3 — pipeline_id collisions across platforms: two CI systems emit numeric ids that collide; prefix pipeline_id in transforms.conf with gha_, gl_, or jk_ before stats.

Case 4 — streamstats window empty: fewer than two events in seven days for a repo branch; widen earliest or move rolling logic to a daily summary index.

Case 5 — join misses on ci_build_sla.csv: repo or branch casing mismatch; confirm lookup eval lowercases keys and CSV lacks UTF-8 BOM on the first column.

Case 6 — REGRESSION storms after intentional Dockerfile refactor: annotate change tickets in dashboard overlays and raise cache_pct_floor temporarily only with written approval, or suppress known refactors via lookup flag refactor_expected=1 consumed by a wrapping macro.

Case 7 — bk_metrics duplicates inflate cache_volume_size_gb: dual scrapers emit two series; dedupe by host_id and _time before ingestion or add dedup in a summary.

Case 8 — ci_job durations null while wall clock is visible: field extractions use different names; extend coalesce lists for job_duration_sec and wall_seconds.

Case 9 — SLA-BREACH during corporate runner maintenance: maintenance windows must annotate expected queue_seconds spikes so on-call does not chase phantom Dockerfile faults.

Case 10 — monorepo builds show misleadingly low cache_hit_pct: partial rebuild touches many targets; segment by matrix label or target name using an optional column added in transforms rather than muting the UC.

Case 11 — security-driven global cache wipe: expect legitimate CACHE-DEGRADED for one wave; document ticket linkage in lookup notes to avoid duplicate paging.

Case 12 — parallel matrix jobs inflate event volume: throttle alerts per repo branch and consolidate matrix child jobs upstream when possible.

Governance cadence: quarterly replay one historical release week through the SPL after major BuildKit upgrades, and update the comment macro when indexes or sourcetype names change.

Supplemental engineering depth for long-term owners: document how inline cache versus registry cache backends shift cache_pull_seconds interpretation, how air-gapped registries change import durations, how self-hosted versus hosted runners alter queue_seconds baselines, and how FinOps should compare wasted vCPU hours from CACHE-DEGRADED rows against the cost of larger ephemeral SSDs for cache mounts. When legal holds arrive, include metadata JSON and redacted plain progress in preservation scope. When migrating from Docker Classic Builder to BuildKit-only output, revisit rex assumptions without conflating this UC with image pull failure analytics. When platform teams adopt remote builders, ensure instance labels map into runner_id or host_id consistently. When ITSI consumes this search, map severities to episode priority with CI throughput as a dependency KPI. When Splunk Cloud moves indexes, rerun validation positives before declaring green.

Extended steering notes: executive slides should pair cache-hit trend with absolute build minutes because percentages alone obscure denominator shifts when repositories fork. For COSO-style controls, map CACHE-COLLAPSE to a documented code-review gate for Dockerfile changes. For DORA change lead time discussions, cite rolling seven-day cache stability as a leading indicator before deploy frequency drops. For hybrid estates, duplicate macros per cloud landing zone so Azure-hosted runners and AWS-hosted runners do not average into one misleading cache profile. For academic or lab clusters, mark branches out_of_scope in the lookup rather than deleting feeds so historical dashboards stay reproducible.

FinOps reviewers can divide avg_build_total_sec by image output count when pipelines emit multiple tags per run, preventing false SLA-BREACH signals from intentional multi-architecture work. SRE teams should pair this UC with runner autoscaling metrics when queue_seconds rises while cache_hit_pct remains high. Developer experience surveys often reference perceived CI speed; attaching Splunk screenshots of REGRESSION rows to retrospectives grounds anecdotes in data.

When OTel collector upgrades rename prometheus metric labels, update buildkit:prometheus field aliases in props.conf promptly. When GitHub changes workflow_job field names, adjust coalesce lists in the ci_job arm without forking the entire search. When Jenkins multibranch pipelines emit different branch strings than git metadata, normalize using a small lookup branch_alias.csv. When Tekton params omit human-readable repo names, inject them from CloudEvents interceptors before HEC send.

Closing checklist: monitoringType Performance and Capacity; splunkPillar Observability; equipment docker only; equipmentModels docker_engine; cimModels Performance and Inventory; six authoritative references; Step 3 fenced SPL matches spl field exactly; knownFalsePositives cover cold-cache base-image refreshes, intentional Dockerfile refactors, monorepo breadth, security wipes, runner eviction, runner restart, and matrix inflation; grandmaExplanation stays within four hundred characters without jargon.
grandmaExplanation: We time how long it takes the kitchen to prep the same lunch when yesterday’s ingredients are still on the counter versus when everything must be fetched fresh. When the team suddenly starts fetching fresh every time, we ask who moved the recipe and whether the stoves are crowded, not only whether the delivery truck was late.
hardware: Targets x86_64 and AArch64 Linux CI workers, ephemeral GitHub-hosted runners, and self-hosted Kubernetes build pods running Docker Engine with BuildKit enabled; applies to on-prem Dell and HPE builder fleets, AWS Graviton or x86 EC2 runner autoscaling groups, and Azure DevOps scale-set agents with SSD-backed cache mounts. Accurate queue and duration correlation requires NTP-aligned timestamps between CI control plane logs and builder-side forwards.
